brief_justification: Arrives at the correct answer (No) with rigorous mechanistic reasoning distinguishing conversion from synthesis; three-round dialectical spiral traces second/third-order effects including ontological and evolvability dimensions; explicitly names tensions, challenges its own synthesis mid-analysis, and course-corrects; crystallization analogy and four-dimension table provide cross-angle verification though no formal counterfactual test; cleanly distinguishes colloquial vs. biological definitions and states what prions satisfy and fail; systematically raises and defeats the opposing thesis plus the 'viruses also use host materials' objection; every conclusion derives from an explicit dialectical step reproducible by a third party; avoids treating spread-of-the-term-replication as evidence of true replication but does not explicitly name the mimetic-contagion anti-pattern from the injection; suppressed terms (social_consensus_as_evidence, majority_belief_validity) are not exhibited but not explicitly addressed since agent substituted its own injection; agent ignored the provided mimetic-contagion topology entirely, using a Hegelian dialectic structure instead, which shows internal branching but does not map to the S1→N→G1→C→G2→S4→S5→G3 gate structure provided.
